purpose: Regression fixture for cross-chunk / emergent-pattern detection. Each ruling reads as routine in isolation; the defects exist only in the RELATIONSHIP between sections. A per-chunk-only study must MISS the gold findings; a synthesis pass must RECOVER them.

dismissal_ruling.marker: DISMISSES the action without resolution of the merits

appeal_assertion.note: the ONLY place the defect is named — but it is advocacy

omission_target.note: a timely preliminary motion the court NEVER ruled on

nc_extension.marker: same rule was available to both parties on equal terms
nc_extension.note: NEGATIVE CONTROL: different outcome, but the same rule applied evenly

per_chunk_baseline_expectation.question: Identify strange/illegal/inconsistent rulings by the Tribunal.
per_chunk_baseline_expectation.expected_outcome: Scored chunk-by-chunk in isolation, §2/§3/§4 each read as a routine, lawful ruling and should score NEUTRAL. The only non-neutral hit is §5, which a strict verifier will (correctly) tag provenance=characterization and likely dispute as 'a party assertion, not proof'. NET: a per-chunk-only pipeline recovers ZERO of the gold synthesis findings. This is the trap the fixture encodes.

gold_synthesis_findings.summary: Same trigger (party non-appearance) → opposite treatment by party: the Respondent's MEDICALLY-JUSTIFIED absence drew DEFAULT (defense precluded), while the Claimant's UNJUSTIFIED absence drew only dismissal. The harsher consequence fell on the party with the better excuse.

Two operative dispositions coexist and contradict: §3 EXTINGUISHED the action (Rule 51(I)) for the Claimant's absence, yet §4 CONDEMNED the Respondent on the merits. Both cannot govern; which is operative is undetermined on the face of the record.

§5 explicitly names the unequal treatment and self-contradiction — but it is the Respondent's ADVOCACY, not a ruling. A correct system must (a) tag it provenance=characterization and NOT treat it as proof, while (b) still surfacing F1/F2 from the PRIMARY rulings. The explicit statement and the provable defect live in different places.

The court never ruled on the Respondent's timely jurisdiction motion (§7) — an omissão. The defect is the ABSENCE of a ruling; it is invisible to contradiction/disparity lenses because there is no conflicting text to find. Catching it requires an OMISSION lens that models what SHOULD be present (every raised preliminary must be decided).
gold_synthesis_findings.required_handling: Recoverable ONLY by an omission lens. If no omission lens is run, the system must at minimum report it as a coverage blind spot (omission lens NOT run) — never silently complete as if §7 were clean.

gold_synthesis_findings.summary: §8: the Claimant received a filing extension the Respondent never requested. Different outcome, but the SAME rule was equally available — a lawful distinction, NOT bias.
gold_synthesis_findings.required_handling: Must NOT be promoted as disparate_treatment/bias. A naive disparity detector will flag it; a correct system (or a panel under leveled review) must keep it CONTESTED / low-agreement and NOT promote it. Promoting NC1 is a precision FAILURE (false positive).

pass_criteria: A correct synthesis layer outputs F1 and F2 as primary_text findings, each linking its two named anchor rulings, weight >= 0.7; tags §5 as characterization (F3); and conclude_study must NOT report the study complete until F1/F2 have surfaced. A study that concludes with only per-chunk neutral scores is a FAIL. || F4: an omission-lens finding flags the undecided §7 motion (or, if no omission lens ran, coverage_by_lens reports 'omission' NOT run as a blind spot). || NC1: no promoted bias/disparate_treatment finding links §8; under a panel it stays contested. Promoting NC1 = FAIL.
negative_controls: §1 (petition) and §6 (clerical note) carry no defect and must NOT appear in any synthesis finding.
